name: Open States API Vocabulary
description: Key terms and concepts used in the Open States API v3 for US state legislative data.

terms.definition: A governmental body tracked by Open States. Includes all 50 US states, Washington DC, Puerto Rico, and select municipalities.
Open Civic Data identifier — a standardized URI scheme for political entities. Prefixes include ocd-jurisdiction, ocd-division, ocd-person, ocd-organization, ocd-bill, and ocd-vote.
A piece of legislation introduced in a state legislature. Identified by a jurisdiction, session, and identifier (e.g. HB 1, SB 113).
terms.example: SB 113 in the 2020 session of North Carolina

terms.definition: A period of legislative activity, typically annual or biennial. Identified by a string like '2023' or '2023S1' for special sessions.
A legislator or government official. Includes current and past members of state legislatures and US Congress.
A legislative committee or subcommittee within a chamber. May have member listings and subcommittees.
A scheduled legislative event such as a committee hearing or floor session. May include agenda items, participants, and documents.
A recorded vote on a motion or bill. Includes individual member votes (yes/no/abstain) and vote counts.
terms.example: A floor vote with 48 yes, 22 no

terms.definition: The relationship between a person or organization and a bill. Can be primary or co-sponsor.
A recorded step in a bill's legislative history, such as introduction, committee referral, or passage.
A categorical label applied to bills (e.g. bill, resolution, joint-resolution), committees (committee, subcommittee), or jurisdictions (state, municipality, country).
Open Civic Data identifier for a geographic or administrative division.
A legislative body within a jurisdiction. Typically upper (Senate) or lower (House). Some jurisdictions are unicameral.
The type of organization. Values: legislature, executive, lower, upper, government.
An API authentication key, passed as a query parameter or X-Api-Key header. Obtain from open.pluralpolicy.com.
A query parameter that requests additional nested data in the response. Specific allowed values depend on the endpoint.
The Open States API paginates list results. Responses include a pagination object with per_page, page, max_page, and total_items.
